---
title: "Phase C вҖ” Interactive simulator"
subtitle: "Live Monte Carlo on parameters of your choosing"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: false
    css: lib/phase-styles.css
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <script src="lib/phase-layout.js" defer></script>
---

Every slider on this page kicks off a fresh Monte Carlo in your
browser вҖ” no server round-trip, no artifact regeneration. The price
model is Geometric Brownian Motion ([Phase A](phase-a.qmd) В§1) with
an optional Merton jump-diffusion overlay; leave the jump sliders at
zero for pure GBM. Retirement flow is deterministic. The hedge is
specified by the **raw inventory state** $(k, C_{\mathrm{basis}})$ вҖ”
kVCM tokens on hand plus their sunk USD cost вҖ” so inventory acquired
earlier at a different price can be studied directly, lifting Phase B's
"bought today at $S_0$" assumption.

{{< include _alpha-bridge.qmd >}}

{{< include _beta-bridge.qmd >}}

{{< include _glossary.qmd >}}

### Notation

Phase A symbols carry over verbatim ([В§1вҖ“В§5](phase-a.qmd)); the
Phase-C-specific rows are $\tau$, $J_\tau$, $k$, $C_{\mathrm{basis}}$,
$k_{\mathrm{left}}$, and $\Pi_{\mathrm{cust}}$.

| Symbol | Meaning | Units |
| --- | --- | --- |
| $S_t$ | kVCM / USD spot price at time $t$ | USD / kVCM |
| $S_0$ | starting spot price | USD / kVCM |
| $\pi_0$ | starting USD protocol price per tonne of COвӮӮ | USD / tonne |
| $P := \pi_0 / S_0$ | protocol constant: kVCM needed per tonne | kVCM / tonne |
| $\mu$ | GBM drift (annualised) | 1 / year |
| $\sigma^2$ | GBM variance (annualised); $\sigma = \sqrt{\sigma^2}$ | 1 / year |
| $\lambda_J$ | Merton jump intensity (expected jumps per year) | 1 / year |
| $\mu_J$ | mean of the log-jump $\log J \sim N(\mu_J, \sigma_J^2)$ | dimensionless |
| $\sigma_J$ | SD of the log-jump | dimensionless |
| $\lambda_{\mathrm{day}}$ | retirement flow specified to the UI | tonnes / day |
| $\lambda = 365 \cdot \lambda_{\mathrm{day}}$ | same flow, internal year unit | tonnes / year |
| $T$ | horizon (days in UI, years internally) | days / years |
| $N := \lambda \cdot T$ | total tonnes retired over $[0, T]$ | tonnes |
| $k$ | pre-purchased inventory | kVCM |
| $C_{\mathrm{basis}}$ | sunk cost of that inventory | USD |
| $N \cdot P$ | tokens needed to meet total demand | kVCM |
| $\tau := \min(1,\; k / (N \cdot P))$ | fraction of horizon the inventory covers | dimensionless |
| $k_{\mathrm{left}} := \max(0,\; k - N \cdot P)$ | leftover tokens at horizon (over-hedge) | kVCM |
| $I_T := \int_0^T S_t\,dt$ | full-horizon time-integral of the price | (USD/kVCM)В·year |
| $J_\tau := \int_{\tau T}^{T} S_t\,dt$ | tail integral over the uncovered-by-inventory window | (USD/kVCM)В·year |
| $\beta \in [0,1]$ | quota-share cession of the custom book's stochastic residual (В§3d) | dimensionless |
| $\theta \geq 0$ | counterparty risk-load multiplier (В§3d) | dimensionless |
| $\pi$ | up-front syndication premium; scalar derived from the MC moments of this run | USD |
| $R_{\mathrm{fee}} = f \cdot P \cdot \lambda \cdot I_T$ | fee-based revenue | USD |
| $\Pi_{\mathrm{b2b}} = Q \cdot N - P \cdot \lambda \cdot I_T$ | back-to-back principal P&L | USD |
| $\Pi_{\mathrm{cust}} = Q \cdot N - C_{\mathrm{basis}} - P \cdot \lambda \cdot J_\tau + k_{\mathrm{left}} \cdot S_T$ | custom-inventory principal P&L | USD |
| $\Pi_{\mathrm{ret}} = (Q \cdot N - C_{\mathrm{basis}}) + (1-\beta) \cdot (\Pi_{\mathrm{cust}} - (Q \cdot N - C_{\mathrm{basis}})) + \pi$ | retained P&L after syndication (В§3d) | USD |
| $Q^* = (1+f) \cdot P \cdot S_0 \cdot (e^{\mu T}-1)/(\mu T)$ | break-even quote equating $\mathbb{E}[\Pi_{\mathrm{b2b}}] = \mathbb{E}[R_{\mathrm{fee}}]$ | USD / tonne |

:::: {.pc-section}

::: {.pc-controls}
### Price process

Sets the four numbers that define the GBM model of the token price.
$\pi_0$ is the carbon price expressed in **USD per tonne** and $P$ is
the same price expressed in **kVCM per tonne** (the protocol constant
вҖ” how many tokens are burned to retire one tonne); together they imply
today's spot $S_0 = \pi_0 / P$. $\mu$ and $\sigma^2$ are the
annualised expected return and variance of the log-price.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof pi0 = Inputs.range([0, 500], { step: 1, value: 125, label: "ПҖвӮҖ (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof P = Inputs.range([100, 25000], { step: 10, value: 2160, label: "P (kVCM / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof mu = Inputs.range([-1, 1], { step: 0.005, value: -0.1, label: "drift Ој (annualised)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sigma2 = Inputs.range([0, 3], { step: 0.005, value: 0.06, label: "variance ПғВІ (annualised)" })
```

### Jumps (Merton)

Optional discrete shocks layered on top of the GBM. $\lambda_J$ is the
expected number of jumps per year; when a jump arrives the log-price
receives an independent $N(\mu_J, \sigma_J^2)$ shock. The drift is
**compensated** ($\mu$ absorbs $-\lambda_J \kappa$ with
$\kappa = e^{\mu_J + \sigma_J^2/2} - 1$) so $\mathbb{E}[S_t] = S_0 e^{\mu t}$
stays put regardless of the jump settings вҖ” jumps widen the
distribution without relocating it. Leaving $\lambda_J = 0$ collapses
to pure GBM.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaJ = Inputs.range([0, 50], { step: 0.1, value: 20, label: "jump intensity О»_J (/yr)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof muJ = Inputs.range([-0.5, 0.5], { step: 0.01, value: -0.05, label: "mean log-jump Ој_J" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sigmaJ = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.35, label: "log-jump SD Пғ_J" })
```

### Demand & inventory

Retirement flow $\lambda$ (tonnes per calendar day) plus the raw
starting inventory state $(k,\, C_{\mathrm{basis}})$. The derived
card **coverage $\tau = \min(1,\, k / (N \cdot P))$** вҖ” fraction of
the horizon that the inventory lasts under deterministic demand вҖ” is
the number that controls the custom book. Three regimes:

- **Under-hedged ($\tau < 1$).** Inventory runs out at $t = \tau T$;
  randomness lives in the tail integral $J_\tau =
  \int_{\tau T}^{T} S_t\,dt$. The loss tail is *unbounded* (high
  prices) but *softened* (averaging effect of an integrated
  log-normal).
- **Exactly matched ($\tau = 1$, $k = N \cdot P$).** Custom P&L is
  $Q \cdot N - C_{\mathrm{basis}}$ вҖ” deterministic (reproduces Phase B В§3a).
- **Over-hedged ($\tau = 1$, $k > N \cdot P$).** Leftover $k_{\mathrm{left}} =
  k - N \cdot P$ tokens are marked to spot at $S_T$. The loss tail
  is *bounded* (prices can't go below zero) but the gain tail now
  carries the heavy raw log-normal mass. Different *kind* of risk, not
  just different amount.

Raising $C_{\mathrm{basis}}$ shifts the custom book's distribution
leftward by the same amount without touching its shape, SD, VaR, or
CVaR вҖ” the basis is a sunk cost.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaDay = Inputs.range([0, 50], { step: 0.1, value: 1, label: "retirements (tonnes / day)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof kPre = Inputs.range([0, 2000000], { step: 1000, value: 300000, label: "initial inventory (kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof cBasis = Inputs.range([0, 1000000], { step: 100, value: 21000, label: "inventory basis (USD, total)" })
```

### Contract

Horizon (in calendar days for UI convenience; converted internally to
years) and the two business levers: the fixed USD quote per tonne $Q$
of the principal book, and the proportional fee $f$ of the fee book.
Doubling $f$ doubles both the fee book's mean and its SD, leaving
Sharpe invariant; $f$ does not touch the principal books.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Tdays = Inputs.range([30, 1095], { step: 5, value: 365, label: "horizon T (days)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Q = Inputs.range([0, 1000], { step: 0.5, value: 180, label: "fixed quote Q (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof fee = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "fee rate f" })
```

### Syndication (В§3d)

Quota-share cession of the custom book's stochastic residual to a
third-party counterparty. $\beta$ is the fraction ceded; $\theta$ is
the counterparty's risk-load (0 = actuarially fair premium). The
premium mode selects the risk measure Оё multiplies вҖ” `sharpe`
multiplies $\theta \cdot \mathrm{SD}$, `cvar` multiplies $\theta \cdot
\mathrm{SD} \cdot \phi(\Phi^{-1}(0.95))/0.05 \approx \theta \cdot
\mathrm{SD} \cdot 2.063$. Variance of the retained book collapses as
$(1-\beta)^2$ on top of the $(1-\tau)^2$ collapse already bought by
internal coverage.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof beta = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0, label: "cession ОІ" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof theta = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0, label: "counterparty load Оё" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof premiumMode = Inputs.radio(["sharpe", "cvar"], { value: "sharpe", label: "premium mode" })
```

### Monte Carlo

Knobs for the simulator itself вҖ” how many random trajectories, how
many time-steps per trajectory, and the PRNG seed. These are
**statistical** controls (more paths = tighter estimates), not
business controls.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof nPaths = Inputs.select([2000, 5000, 10000, 20000, 40000], { value: 5000, label: "paths" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof nSteps = Inputs.select([50, 100, 200, 365], { value: 100, label: "steps / path" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof seed = Inputs.range([1, 9999], { step: 1, value: 42, label: "PRNG seed" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
mcResetButton = {
  const defaults = {
    pi0: 125, P: 2160, mu: -0.1, sigma2: 0.06,
    lambdaJ: 20, muJ: -0.05, sigmaJ: 0.35,
    lambdaDay: 1, kPre: 300000, cBasis: 21000,
    Tdays: 365, Q: 180, fee: 0.4,
    beta: 0, theta: 0, premiumMode: "sharpe",
    nPaths: 5000, nSteps: 100, seed: 42,
  };
  const views = {
    pi0: viewof pi0, P: viewof P, mu: viewof mu, sigma2: viewof sigma2,
    lambdaJ: viewof lambdaJ, muJ: viewof muJ, sigmaJ: viewof sigmaJ,
    lambdaDay: viewof lambdaDay, kPre: viewof kPre, cBasis: viewof cBasis,
    Tdays: viewof Tdays, Q: viewof Q, fee: viewof fee,
    beta: viewof beta, theta: viewof theta, premiumMode: viewof premiumMode,
    nPaths: viewof nPaths, nSteps: viewof nSteps, seed: viewof seed,
  };
  const wrap = document.createElement("div");
  wrap.className = "pc-reset";
  const btn = document.createElement("button");
  btn.type = "button";
  btn.textContent = "Reset to defaults";
  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    for (const k of Object.keys(defaults)) {
      const el = views[k];
      el.value = defaults[k];
      el.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
    }
  });
  wrap.appendChild(btn);
  return wrap;
}
```

:::

::: {.pc-main}

## Derived quantities

Closed-form summaries of the slider state. $S_0 = \pi_0 / P$ is
today's token spot; $T$ is the horizon in years; $N = \lambda T$ is
total tonnes retired; coverage $\tau$ is the fraction of the horizon
the inventory lasts. The break-even quote
$Q^* = (1+f) \cdot P \cdot S_0 \cdot (e^{\mu T} - 1)/(\mu T)$ is the
one at which the fee and back-to-back books have equal expected P&L.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
derived = {
  const sigma = Math.sqrt(sigma2);
  const S0 = P > 0 ? pi0 / P : 0;
  const T = Tdays / 365;
  const lambda = lambdaDay * 365;
  const N = lambda * T;
  const tokensNeeded = N * P;
  const coverageDays = (lambda > 0 && P > 0) ? kPre / (lambda * P) * 365 : Infinity;
  const coverageFrac = tokensNeeded > 0 ? Math.min(1, kPre / tokensNeeded) : 0;
  const impliedCost = kPre > 0 ? cBasis / kPre : 0;
  const QStarRatio = expm1OverX(mu * T);
  const QStar = (1 + fee) * P * S0 * QStarRatio;
  return { sigma, S0, T, lambda, N, tokensNeeded, coverageDays, coverageFrac, impliedCost, QStar };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="phase-c-derived">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">Пғ (volatility)</div><div class="value">${derived.sigma.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">SвӮҖ = ПҖвӮҖ / P (USD / kVCM)</div><div class="value">${"$" + derived.S0.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">T (years)</div><div class="value">${derived.T.toFixed(3)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">N = О»В·T (tonnes over horizon)</div><div class="value">${derived.N.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">tokens needed NВ·P</div><div class="value">${derived.tokensNeeded.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">coverage from inventory</div><div class="value">${(derived.coverageFrac * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">inventory runs out at (days)</div><div class="value">${isFinite(derived.coverageDays) ? Math.min(derived.coverageDays, Tdays).toFixed(1) : "вҲһ"}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">implied basis per token</div><div class="value">${kPre > 0 ? "$" + derived.impliedCost.toFixed(3) : "вҖ”"}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">break-even Q* (USD / tonne)</div><div class="value">${"$" + derived.QStar.toFixed(3)}</div></div>
</div>`
```

`implied basis per token` compares $C_{\mathrm{basis}} / k$ to $S_0$:
above $S_0$ is an unrealised loss on the existing inventory, below is
an unrealised gain. It shifts only the location of the custom book's
P&L distribution, not its shape.

<details>
<summary>What the simulator computes, step by step</summary>

Every slider change triggers the following sequence. The simulator
is a direct in-browser port of `src/rng.ts`, `src/gbm.ts`,
`src/moments.ts`, and `src/risk.ts`; nothing writes to disk or calls
the network.

1. **Seed the PRNG.** Mulberry32 from the `seed` slider; BoxвҖ“Muller
   converts uniform pairs into standard normals.
2. **Simulate $n_{\mathrm{paths}}$ GBM (or jump-GBM) trajectories.**
   Log-GBM increment $S_{t+dt} = S_t \cdot \exp\!\big((\mu -
   \tfrac12 \sigma^2 - \lambda_J \kappa)\,dt + \sigma\sqrt{dt}\,Z +
   \sum_{j=1}^{N_{t+dt} - N_t} Y_j\big)$, with $N$ a Poisson process
   of intensity $\lambda_J$, $Y_j \sim N(\mu_J, \sigma_J^2)$, and
   $\kappa = e^{\mu_J + \sigma_J^2/2} - 1$ the Merton compensation.
   $\lambda_J = 0$ collapses to pure GBM.
3. **Accumulate integrals.** $I_T = \int_0^T S_t\,dt$ and the tail
   integral $J_\tau = \int_{\tau T}^T S_t\,dt$ by the trapezoid
   rule.
4. **Apply each book's formula.** Fee: $f P \lambda I_T$.
   Back-to-back: $Q N - P \lambda I_T$. Custom: $Q N -
   C_{\mathrm{basis}} - P \lambda J_\tau + k_{\mathrm{left}} S_T$.
5. **Summarise.** Mean, SD, 95/99 VaR and CVaR, loss probability,
   Sharpe-like ratio.
6. **Keep ~20 paths for plotting.** The rest contribute only their
   scalar P&L.

</details>

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
import {
  expm1OverX, xTicksForHorizon, simulate, summarise,
} from "./lib/compiled/src/core/index.js"
```

## Monte Carlo run

Live output: a run card (paths, wall time, coverage, leftover
tokens), a summary table, histograms, and sampled price paths. Wall
time stays sub-second at 5,000вҖ“10,000 paths; at 40,000 the bottleneck
is re-plotting, not simulation.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## What raising $\lambda_J$ does

The compensated drift absorbs the jump mean, so every book's
expected P&L is invariant in $(\lambda_J, \mu_J, \sigma_J)$ вҖ” the
black mean rule on each histogram stays put. What does move: SD, VaR,
and CVaR all widen; $\mu_J < 0$ tilts skew toward longer left tails
on the principal books (right tail on fee, its mirror); sample price
paths pick up visible discrete drops. Set $\lambda_J = 0$ to recover
pure GBM bit-for-bit.
:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
run = {
  const inputs = {
    S0: derived.S0, mu, sigma: derived.sigma, P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee, kPre, cBasis,
    beta, premiumLoad: theta, premiumMode,
    lambdaJ, muJ, sigmaJ,
    nPaths, nSteps, seed,
    keepPaths: 20,
  };
  const t0 = performance.now();
  const raw = simulate(inputs);
  const elapsedMs = performance.now() - t0;
  return {
    inputs,
    ...raw,
    fee: summarise(raw.feeSamples),
    principal: summarise(raw.principalSamples),
    b2b: summarise(raw.b2bSamples),
    retained: summarise(raw.retainedSamples),
    elapsedMs,
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="phase-c-derived">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">paths simulated</div><div class="value">${nPaths.toLocaleString()}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">wall time</div><div class="value">${run.elapsedMs.toFixed(1)} ms</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">coverage this run</div><div class="value">${(run.tauFrac * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">leftover tokens at T</div><div class="value">${run.tokensLeftover.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">cession ОІ</div><div class="value">${(beta * 100).toFixed(0)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">premium ПҖ received</div><div class="value">${(run.premium >= 0 ? "$" : "вҲ’$") + Math.abs(run.premium).toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">retained CVaRвӮүвӮ…</div><div class="value">${(run.retained.cvar95 >= 0 ? "$" : "вҲ’$") + Math.abs(run.retained.cvar95).toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">SD reduction vs. principal</div><div class="value">${run.principal.sd > 0 ? (100 * (1 - run.retained.sd / run.principal.sd)).toFixed(1) + "%" : "вҖ”"}</div></div>
</div>`
```

### Summary statistics

Three books per draw: **fee** ($R_{\mathrm{fee}} = f \cdot P \cdot
\lambda \cdot I_T$), **principal (back-to-back)**
($\Pi_{\mathrm{b2b}} = Q \cdot N - P \cdot \lambda \cdot I_T$;
Phase B's `principal_3b`), and **principal (custom inventory)**
($\Pi_{\mathrm{cust}} = Q \cdot N - C_{\mathrm{basis}} - P \cdot
\lambda \cdot J_\tau + k_{\mathrm{left}} \cdot S_T$). The
back-to-back row is the reference against which the custom row's
hedging is measured.

| column | meaning |
| --- | --- |
| $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$ (mc) | sample mean of the book's P&L |
| SD | sample standard deviation |
| $\pm \mathrm{CI}_{95}$ | 95% half-width of the mean ($1.96 \cdot \mathrm{SD}/\sqrt{n}$) |
| $\mathrm{VaR}_{95} / \mathrm{VaR}_{99}$ | loss threshold exceeded with 5% / 1% probability |
| $\mathrm{CVaR}_{95} / \mathrm{CVaR}_{99}$ | mean loss *inside* the worst 5% / 1% tail |
| $\mathbb{P}[\Pi < 0]$ | probability of a losing realisation |
| Sharpe | $\mathbb{E}[\Pi] / \mathrm{SD}[\Pi]$ |

P&L is total USD over $[0, T]$ вҖ” divide by $N = \lambda T$ for a
per-tonne figure. What to watch as sliders move:

- **Custom row** is the one that responds to everything вҖ” $Q$ shifts
  its mean, $\sigma$ widens its tails, $k$ changes its shape (via
  $\tau$), $C_{\mathrm{basis}}$ translates only its location.
- **Back-to-back row** depends only on $Q$ and the price process; if
  the custom row converges to it as $k \to 0$ and $C_{\mathrm{basis}}
  \to 0$, the Phase B identity $\Pi_{\mathrm{cust}} \to
  \Pi_{\mathrm{b2b}}$ is wired correctly.
- **$\tau = 100\%$** collapses the custom row's SD and CVaR toward
  zero вҖ” the matched-book limit.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
summaryRows = [
  { book: "fee", ...run.fee },
  { book: "principal (custom)", ...run.principal },
  { book: "principal (retained, В§3d)", ...run.retained },
  { book: "principal (back-to-back)", ...run.b2b },
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(summaryRows, {
  columns: ["book","mean","sd","ci95","var95","var99","cvar95","cvar99","probLoss","sharpe"],
  header: {
    book: "book",
    mean: "E[О ] (mc)",
    sd: "SD",
    ci95: "ВұCI95",
    var95: "VaR95",
    var99: "VaR99",
    cvar95: "CVaR95",
    cvar99: "CVaR99",
    probLoss: "P[О <0]",
    sharpe: "Sharpe",
  },
  format: {
    mean: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "вҲ’$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    sd: x => "$" + x.toFixed(2),
    ci95: x => "Вұ$" + x.toFixed(2),
    var95: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "вҲ’$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    var99: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "вҲ’$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    cvar95: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "вҲ’$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    cvar99: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "вҲ’$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    probLoss: x => x.toFixed(3),
    sharpe: x => x === null ? "вҖ”" : (x >= 0 ? "" : "вҲ’") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(3),
  }
})
```

### P&L distributions

One panel per book. The solid black rule is the MC mean (a converged
MC lands on it); the dashed grey rule is the break-even $\Pi = 0$.

- **Fee.** Non-negative by construction; histogram entirely to the
  right of zero. Right-skewed (inherits $I_T$'s right skew).
- **Principal (custom).** The responsive panel. Low coverage вҶ’ wide,
  left-skewed (back-to-back regime); $\tau = 100\%$ вҶ’ spike
  (matched); over-hedge вҶ’ width returns but with a different shape
  (terminal-$S_T$ exposure).
- **Principal (back-to-back).** Mirror of the fee panel. The custom
  panel collapses onto this one when $k = C_{\mathrm{basis}} = 0$.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pnlRecords = {
  const rows = [];
  const push = (label, arr) => {
    for (let i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) rows.push({ book: label, pnl: arr[i] });
  };
  // Subsample for plotting responsiveness.
  const stride = Math.max(1, Math.floor(run.feeSamples.length / 2500));
  for (let i = 0; i < run.feeSamples.length; i += stride) {
    rows.push({ book: "fee", pnl: run.feeSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "principal (custom)", pnl: run.principalSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "principal (retained, В§3d)", pnl: run.retainedSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "principal (back-to-back)", pnl: run.b2bSamples[i] });
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 900,
  height: 560,
  marginLeft: 80,
  marginRight: 30,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "terminal P&L (USD)", tickFormat: "$~s" },
  y: { label: "empirical density" },
  fy: { label: "book" },
  color: { legend: true, domain: ["fee","principal (custom)","principal (retained, В§3d)","principal (back-to-back)"] },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.ruleX([0], { stroke: "#888", strokeDasharray: "2 4" }),
    Plot.rectY(pnlRecords, Plot.binX({ y: "proportion" }, { x: "pnl", fill: "book", fy: "book", thresholds: 50 })),
    Plot.ruleX([
      { book: "fee", x: run.fee.mean },
      { book: "principal (custom)", x: run.principal.mean },
      { book: "principal (retained, В§3d)", x: run.retained.mean },
      { book: "principal (back-to-back)", x: run.b2b.mean },
    ], { x: "x", fy: "book", stroke: "black", strokeWidth: 2 }),
  ]
})
```

_Solid black: MC-estimated $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$. Dashed grey: $\Pi = 0$. The
custom-principal panel collapses toward a spike as coverage approaches
100% вҖ” that's the В§3a matched book limit. The retained panel sits on
top of the custom panel at $\beta = 0$ and collapses to a spike as
$\beta \to 1$ вҖ” that is the В§3d syndication limit._

### Sample price paths and inventory decay

Twenty sampled GBM trajectories of the spot (grey, with the first
highlighted in orange). The dashed green rule sits at $t = \tau T$,
the time at which pre-purchased inventory is exhausted вҖ” left of the
rule the operator draws from inventory, right of the rule they buy at
spot. The custom book's randomness lives in the right-of-rule region
(plus $S_T$ if over-hedged). At $\tau = 100\%$ the rule sits at or
past $T$; at $\tau = 0$ it sits at $t = 0$. Paths fan out like
$\sigma\sqrt{t}$ вҖ” the visual signature of GBM.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pathRecords = {
  const rows = [];
  const paths = run.sampledPaths;
  const nS = run.inputs.nSteps;
  for (let idx = 0; idx < paths.length; idx++) {
    const path = paths[idx];
    for (let k = 0; k <= nS; k++) {
      rows.push({
        tDays: (k / nS) * Tdays,
        S: path[k],
        id: idx,
      });
    }
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 900,
  height: 340,
  marginLeft: 70,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "t (days)", domain: [0, Tdays], ticks: xTicksForHorizon(Tdays) },
  y: { label: "kVCM spot S_t (USD)", zero: true, tickFormat: "$~s" },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.line(pathRecords, { x: "tDays", y: "S", z: "id", stroke: "#888", strokeOpacity: 0.35 }),
    Plot.line(pathRecords.filter(r => r.id === 0), { x: "tDays", y: "S", stroke: "#c94", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    Plot.ruleX([run.tauFrac * Tdays], { stroke: "#2a7", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 2 }),
  ]
})
```

_Grey: sampled GBM trajectories of the kVCM spot. Orange: first path
highlighted. Green dashed rule: time at which the pre-purchased inventory
is exhausted вҖ” to the right of this line, retirements are sourced at
spot._

:::

::::

For the cross-phase synthesis and a deterministic stress-test tool,
see the [Findings](index.qmd) page.
